2603.16544.title: How often do Answers Change? Estimating Recency Requirements in Question Answering
2603.16544.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) often rely on outdated knowledge when answering time-sensitive questions, leading to confident yet incorrect responses. Without explicit signals indicating whether up-to-date information is required, models struggle to decide when to retrieve external evidence, how to reason about stale facts, and how to rank answers by their validity. Existing benchmarks either periodically refresh answers or rely on fixed templates, but they do not reflect on how frequently answers change or whether a question inherently requires up-to-date information. To address this gap, we introduce a recency-stationarity taxonomy that categorizes questions by how often their answers change and whether this change frequency is time-invariant or context-dependent. Building on this taxonomy, we present RecencyQA, a dataset of 4,031 open-domain questions annotated with recency and stationarity labels. Through human evaluation and empirical analysis, we show that non-stationary questions, i.e., those where context changes the recency requirement, are significantly more challenging for LLMs, with difficulty increasing as update frequency rises. By explicitly modeling recency and context dependence, RecencyQA enables fine-grained benchmarking and analysis of temporal reasoning beyond binary notions of freshness, and provides a foundation for developing recency-aware and context-sensitive question answering systems.
2603.16544.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在回答時間敏感問題時，往往依賴過時的知識，導致自信但不正確的回應。<lb/>如果沒有明確的信號指示是否需要最新的信息，模型在決定何時檢索外部證據、如何推理過時的事實以及如何根據有效性對答案進行排序時會遇到困難。<lb/>現有的基準要麼定期刷新答案，要麼依賴固定模板，但它們並未反映答案變化的頻率或問題是否固有地需要最新的信息。<lb/>為了解決這一空白，我們引入了一種新近性-穩定性分類法，根據答案變化的頻率以及這一變化頻率是否時間不變或依賴於上下文來對問題進行分類。<lb/>基於這一分類法，我們提出了RecencyQA，這是一個包含4,031個開放領域問題的數據集，並附有新近性和穩定性標籤。<lb/>通過人類評估和實證分析，我們顯示非穩定性問題，即那些上下文改變了新近性要求的問題，對LLMs來說顯著更具挑戰性，隨著更新頻率的提高，難度也在增加。<lb/>通過明確建模新近性和上下文依賴性，RecencyQA使得對時間推理的細緻基準測試和分析成為可能，超越了新鮮度的二元概念，並為開發具備新近性意識和上下文敏感的問答系統提供了基礎。
2603.16544.author: Bhawna Piryani et.al.
2603.16544.authors: Bhawna Piryani, Zehra Mert, Adam Jatowt
